report_name: Aflac Japan Data Breach Exposes Data of Up to 4.38 Million Customers

threat_actors.name: Unauthorized Third Party
threat_actors.description: An unidentified, unauthorized third party that gained access to Aflac Life Insurance Japan Ltd. systems between June 15 and June 25, 2026. The actor is presumed to be financially motivated, accessing and exfiltrating sensitive policyholder data, including personal and bank account information.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for financial gain, potentially through selling the data on dark web marketplaces or for use in extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The unauthorized third party accessed files containing highly sensitive customer data, including personal information, policy details, and bank account information, from Aflac Japan's internal systems.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The unauthorized third party exfiltrated sensitive customer data, exposing the information of up to 4.38 million policyholders. The exact method was not disclosed, but data was moved to an attacker-controlled external location.

identities.name: Aflac Life Insurance Japan Ltd.
identities.description: The Japanese subsidiary of Aflac and the primary victim of the data breach. Its systems containing sensitive policyholder data were compromised.

identities.name: Aflac
identities.description: The American parent corporation of Aflac Life Insurance Japan Ltd. The company disclosed the breach to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

identities.name: Aflac Japan Policyholders
identities.description: A class of up to 4.38 million individuals whose personal information, policy details, and bank account numbers were exposed in the data breach.

indicators.name: Anomalous Remote Access Login Behavior
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Remote Access Login Behavior
id: 2b1c8e9f-4d5a-4b6c-8e9f-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects multiple failed remote access logins followed by a success from the same source IP, which could indicate credential abuse or brute-forcing.
  This is a general hunting rule based on the provided article.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/insurance-giant-aflac-discloses-data-breach-after-subsidiary-hack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
level: medium
logsource:
  category: authentication
  product: vpn
detection:
  selection_fail:
    action: 'failure'
  selection_success:
    action: 'success'
  condition: selection_fail and selection_success
indicators.description: Detects patterns of remote access login activity, such as multiple failed logins followed by a success, that could indicate credential abuse. This is based on general hunting hints provided in the report.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Data Egress
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Data Egress
id: 9f8e7d6c-5b4a-4d3c-8b2a-1f2e3d4c5b6a
status: experimental
description: |
  Monitors for unusually large data transfers from internal servers to external IP addresses. 
  This rule requires baselining of normal network traffic to be effective and may generate false positives without proper tuning.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/insurance-giant-aflac-discloses-data-breach-after-subsidiary-hack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
level: low
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    destination_ip_type: external
    bytes_out|gte: 100000000
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects unusually large data transfers from internal servers to external destinations, which could be a sign of data exfiltration. This is based on general hunting hints provided in the report.

indicators.name: Anomalous Cloud File Access Volume
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Cloud File Access Volume by User
id: 7d6c5b4a-3d2c-4b1a-9a8b-7c6d5e4f3a2b
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a single user account accessing an unusually high volume of files in a short period from cloud storage.
  This requires baselining and may indicate data staging or collection prior to exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/insurance-giant-aflac-discloses-data-breach-after-subsidiary-hack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.sharepoint
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: FileAccessed
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a user account accessing an unusually high volume of files from cloud storage, which could indicate data collection. This is based on general hunting hints provided in the report.
